title: Moappa

{{Quote|And if a few of us should burn away… more will take their place.|The Moappa|<ref name="FC" />}}
The '''Moappa''' were one of the [[Sentience/Legends|intelligent]] deep-sea [[Species/Legends|species]] living in the [[Ocean/Legends|oceans]] of [[Dac/Legends|Mon Calamari]]. [[Telepathy/Legends|Telepathic]], they formed a sentient group intelligence from the nearly mindless individuals. There were millions of these small glowing creatures in the worldwide [[Ocean/Legends|ocean]] of Mon Calamari; although individually appearing harmless at first sight, when threatened Moappa could combine into large groups, forming a gigantic group organism to defend themselves. If one Moappa in this group consciousness died, it would be replaced by others.

[[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Kit Fisto/Legends|Kit Fisto]] fought with a group of Moappa during the first [[Standard month/Legends|months]] of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], when they aided the [[Quarren Isolation League/Legends|Quarren Isolation League]] during the [[Battle of Mon Calamari (Clone Wars)/Legends|Battle of Mon Calamari]]. Angry at the arrogance of the [[Mon Calamari/Legends|Mon Calamari]] in claiming the world as their own, the Moappa aided the [[Quarren/Legends|Quarren]] even after Fisto captured the Quarren leadership. After learning of their true nature, Fisto advised the Calamari to find a diplomatic, as opposed to military, solution, and peace was eventually made between the Moappa and the co-inhabitants of their world. [[137 ABY|Over a century later]], under the reign of [[Darth Krayt's Galactic Empire]], the Moappa's home planet was [[Final Protocol|purged]] of all life. The [[Galactic Alliance Remnant]] spearheaded an [[Evacuation of Dac|evacuation]] of the Moappa and the other species.

==Biology and appearance==
Moappa were a primitive [[invertebrate]] [[Ocean/Legends|ocean]]-dwelling [[Species/Legends|species]]. Individual Moappa had a conical head with several small [[Limb/Legends|tentacles]] lining the base, and one large tentacle or tail extruding from its center. They were a light pink color, glowed, and had a delicate appearance.<ref name="FC" />

An individual Moappa was a mindless organism. However, when a few dozen were brought together, their intelligence increased to genius-level. A colony of Moappa could combine into a single unit; sometimes in a [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoid]] form, others as a series of long connected chains of Moappa, with each Moappa locking its tentacles around the head of the next. These combined forms could split into pieces and re-combine, and if Moappa within them were destroyed, others could swim in to fill the gap.<ref name="FC" />

Moappa communicated with each other through [[Telepathy/Legends|telepathy]]; they could also project their thoughts into the minds of other non-Moappa, but were unable to read minds. However, they had a very quick reaction time.<ref name="FC" />

==Society and culture==
The Moappa were a collective consciousness. On their own, they were unintelligent, but when brought together the species developed a sophisticated intellect.<ref name="TCWCG" /> Moappa thought as one organism in groups, but if these were pulled apart, the unified mind shattered back into individual ones. Single Moappa experienced feelings of loneliness.<ref name="FC" />

Though they inhabited the oceans of [[Dac/Legends|Mon Calamari]] with other [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] species, including the [[Mon Calamari/Legends|Mon Calamari]], the Moappa believed Mon Calamari saw themselves as the sole rulers of the world. The Moappa's own belief that the world belonged as much to them as to anyone put them at odds with their fellow inhabitants of the planet.<ref name="FC" /> The diplomacy that opened up between the Mon Calamari and Moappa eventually smoothed this relation over.<ref name="TCWCG" />

==History==
{{Quote|Because the Mon Cal think that this is just their world… But it belongs to ''all'' of us!|Moappa|<ref name="FC" />}}
[[File:MoappaFisto.jpg|thumb|left|200px|The Moappa fight Kit Fisto.]]
The Moappa were longtime inhabitants of Mon Calamari; they had been around since before the Mon Calamari species even evolved.<ref name="FC" />

Due to the Moappa's perception of the Mon Calamari claiming an unrightful dominion over all the [[Planet/Legends|planet]], they joined with the [[Quarren/Legends|Quarren]] [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Separatists]] during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] to bring war to the planet. After the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] destroyed the Quarren command center, the Moappa continued to lead the Quarren in organized attacks on the Mon Calamari. [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Kit Fisto/Legends|Kit Fisto]], sensing a presence in the ocean, set out to find the new Quarren leader.<ref name="FC" />

After interrogating a group of Quarren and learning that they received their orders telepathically, Fisto spoke with the Mon Calamari, who did not know who could do such a thing. In short time, Fisto stumbled upon a colony of the Moappa. When he prodded one, it proceeded to engulf his arm, at which point several other Moappa swarmed him. The others nearby coalesced into a roughly humanoid form and spoke with Fisto. A brief fight ensued, during which Fisto learned of the Moappa's motives and abilities. When he realized they were weaker separately than combined, he used [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] to pull them apart, giving him time to escape.<ref name="FC" />

The Mon Calamari, once they learned of the nature of the Moappa, were concerned that they would not be able to defeat such a species. Fisto, however, advised them to choose a route of diplomacy and strike a deal with the other native species of their world.<ref name="FC" /> When the Clone Wars ended, diplomatic relations opened up between the Mon Calamari and Moappa, and the two species entered a peaceful coexistence.<ref name="TCWCG" />

In [[137 ABY]], the [[Darth Krayt's Galactic Empire|Galactic Empire]] under [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Darth Krayt]] began a [[Genocide on Dac|purge]] of the Mon Calamari as punishment for the species' resistance to the Empire. The Moappa remained largely neutral, but later that year Krayt's successor [[Darth Wyyrlok (III)|Darth Wyyrlok]] ordered the [[Final Protocol|purge]] of all life on the planet. The [[Galactic Alliance Remnant]] began an [[Evacuation of Dac|evacuation]] of the world and made contact with the Moappa, who understood the danger they were in. One [[YZ 3000 tanker]] was assigned to the evacuation of their species, which the Moappa found sufficient to hold enough of them to preserve the group mind. Along with the rest of the world's species, the Moappa were removed to a safe world located by the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]].<ref name="Legacy 47">[[Legacy (2006) 47|''Legacy'' (2006) 47]]</ref>

==Moappa in the galaxy==
{{Quote|We just… hear them in our minds.|Quarren Separatist|<ref name="FC" />}}
A deep-sea species,<ref name="TCWCG" /> millions of Moappa inhabited the oceans of Mon Calamari. The Mon Calamari saw them as a harmless primitive species, and until the Clone Wars were unaware of their true nature.<ref name="FC" /> In 137 ABY, they were relocated from Mon Calamari to a safe planet following the purge of their homeworld's native life.<ref name="Legacy 47" />

For a time during the Clone Wars, the Moappa became one of the leaders of the Separatist-aligned Quarren. They communicated their orders to the Quarren telepathically, and the Quarren neither knew who their leader was or where it could be found.<ref name="FC" /> About a [[Standard month/Legends|month]] after the [[Battle of Mon Calamari (Clone Wars)/Legends|Battle of Mon Calamari]], a Moappa sample was stored in a storage container on [[Count/Legends|Count]] [[Dooku/Legends|Dooku's]] [[Space Station Trenchant/Legends|Trenchant space station]].<ref name="Rogue's Gallery">{{CWACite|3|Rogue's Gallery}}</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
The Moappa were created in the [[Star Wars: Clone Wars Adventures (graphic novels)|''Clone Wars Adventures'']] comic ''[[Fierce Currents]]'', by [[W. Haden Blackman|Haden Blackman]]. They are the seventh intelligent species inhabiting Dac to be described in [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]].
